content:
I’m Not Worried That AI Will Replace Students. I’m Worried They’ll Stop Believing They Can Think Without It.

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Students can’t reason’: Teachers warn AI is fueling a crisis in kids’ ability to think | FortuneI spend a lot of time working with young people, and over the past year I’ve found myself thinking more and more about the role artificial intelligence is beginning to play in education. Not because I think AI is inherently bad. Quite the opposite, actually. I use it almost every day to organize ideas, brainstorm solutions, edit writing, and tackle problems more efficiently. It’s one of the most remarkable technologies I’ve seen in my lifetime.
What concerns me isn’t the technology itself. It’s what happens when young people begin to believe that a machine can think better than they can.
A conversation I had with a middle school student has stayed with me ever since it happened. We were working through a history assignment together, and I noticed he wasn’t really struggling with the material. He understood the concepts, but every time it came time to write an answer in his own words, he froze. After a few moments of silence, he looked at me and said, almost matter-of-factly, “Just ChatGPT it.”
The comment caught me off guard. It wasn’t said as a joke, and it certainly wasn’t said with any sense of guilt. To him, using…
